topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that felt too loud in the silence of my room. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in fifteen years—the same sneering grin, the same cheap sunglasses. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, then retreated, trembling slightly. A wave of intense, cold nausea washed over me, making my stomach clench tight. I stared at the screen, the familiar sting behind my eyes making the room swim. *Why now?* The thought echoed hollowly in my skull. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing like a gunshot. I needed to move, to find a place where this digital ghost couldn't reach me.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone slowly, the glass cool against my palm. The name flashed up: Mark. My chest tightened instantly, a heavy, suffocating pressure that made it difficult to draw a full breath. I watched the profile picture, and a strange, acidic burn started low in my throat. I didn't want to respond, but a strange compulsion—a desperate need to know, to gauge the distance between us—pulled my thumb toward the button. My jaw locked so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. When I finally closed the app, I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my small apartment. Each step felt clumsy, weighted down by an invisible anchor. The air felt thin, and I just wanted to sink into the floor.

[story 3]

The chime sounded, and I flinched, dropping the mug I was holding. The ceramic shattered on the hardwood floor, the sound sharp and unforgiving. It was him. That familiar, unwelcome presence materialized on my feed. A profound emptiness settled in my core, a vast, echoing void where warmth used to reside. I didn't look at the screen again; instead, I focused on the dust motes dancing in the sunlight slanting through the window, watching them drift aimlessly. My hands began to shake uncontrollably, and I pressed them against my temples, trying to physically contain the sudden, overwhelming urge to flee the entire interaction. The silence in the room was deafening, amplifying the frantic thumping of my own pulse.
